feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: I want to learn SAP Business One, Web client for my daily tasks. Where can I start?
entry.content:
We  recommend to access our Exploring SAP Business One, Web Client learning journey learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey comprises of four courses, which will enable users to effectively use SAP Business One, Web client for their daily tasks, leverage the different tools to boost their productivity and collaboration within the company, and use powerful analytic tools offered in the Web client.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Effectively use and personalize SAP Business One, Web client for managing daily business processes
  • Leverage the integration capabilities with Microsoft applications to facilitate collaboration
  • Manage master data and activities to increase customer engagement
  • Handle the sales and procurement processes to increase business productivity
  • Use powerful analytics offered in the Web client to enhance visibility on the business performance

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

We appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

entry.title: SAP Business One Web Client Order-to-Cash Pre-sales - Demo _ 2024 Partner Summit
entry.content:
This video shows the current release 2402 in SAP Business One WebClient and its capabilities, it is a pre-sales showcase, a step-by-step click-through based on the workflows of the personas:Sales Manager > Sales Representative > Warehouse Manager > Accountant
Step-by-step Video:  https://d.dam.sap.com/s/p/a/12PPXxn?rc=10&doi=SAP1080856

This map can be viewed in detail via FIGMA: https://www.figma.com/file/xaN0JRo57aJvykgsNLNp2J/Order-to-cash-flow?type=design&node-id=0%3A1&mode=design&t=JL5sJKtSMb9mNdN9-1

 

Summarised Highlights for Marketing Documents
Until 2402:

• Long Text  (opening and Closing Text)
• Support “Text” and “Subtotal” line types
• Linked list Views

Get started with SAP Business One Web Client!
https://www.sap.com/products/erp/business-one/get-started.html

entry.title: Series period does not match current period 65171 SAP Business One
entry.content:
Dear all,

When I am trying to upload the Purchase Blanket Agreement data with DTW then I am facing error like

"Created Failed 241000009 Series period does not match current period 65171 "

SAP Business One 

Issue : Purchase Blanket Agreement DTW data

Kind regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: What’s happening in SAP Business One Support?
entry.content:
In today's blog post we wanted to take a moment and share a quick update on what we've been working on over in the SAP Business One Support organization. It's been a busy time for us - with lots of exciting changes and initiatives in the works.

Stay tuned and keep reading to discover what we've been up to...

Interactive Webinars

First off, we kicked off a new initiative featuring engaging, interactive sessions led by our Support Engineers for our SAP Business One Partners. In April, we rolled out the session "Maximizing your SAP Business One Support Experience as a Partner." We offered this session in four different languages - English, Chinese, Spanish, and Portuguese - over four different sessions.

We were thrilled with the turnout of about 250 attendees! It's clear that there's a real interest for these types of events. That's why we're excited to announce that registration is now open for our next session, "SAP Business One Version for SAP HANA - Database Administration Best Practices." We'll be conducting this session in English on Tuesday, June 25 at 9AM EST, 2PM GMT, 3PM CET. Some of the topics we'll be covering include:

  • SDC (Single Container System) and MDC (Multi-tenant Database Containers) and the difference between the two approaches.
  • Administration and housekeeping tasks, focusing on best practices for improved performance.
  • Guidance on finding SAP HANA database-related content, helping participants navigate the available resources effectively.


The speaker for our next session will be none other than our HANA Product Area Lead, Sagar Dixit. We encourage an interactive discussion, so feel free to ask Sagar questions during the session.

Interested in joining? Click here to register for our next session: "SAP Business One Version for SAP HANA - Database Administration Best Practices."

And that's not all – keep an eye out for future sessions addressing a variety of topics like Fixed Assets, B1i, Service Layer and more. These will all be delivered by topic experts within our Support Organization.

If you missed our first session or would like to revisit it, we've got you covered. You can find the recordings and presentation material in English, Chinese, Spanish, and Portuguese.

Just remember - the presentation material is always in English and exclusive for Partners.

 

Guided Answers

Since our last update, we've released a bunch of new Guided Answers that you can check out.

If you're not quite sure what Guided Answers are, don't worry, we've got a blog post that explains all about them. Guided Answers are a fantastic tool for assisting with troubleshooting, helping you find solutions even quicker. Check out the post here: "Guided Answers – Assisted troubleshooting for even faster solutions."

The latest released GAs include - Working with Inventory Audit Report, Changing the Valuation Method of Items, Electronic File/Document/Report Failed to be Added or Generated

SAP Business One Support has of today released 28 different GAs. Find all of them here: Guided Answers

 

SAP Business One Community

As you might have heard the SAP Community has received a much-needed revamp and moved to a new platform. If you want to inform yourself on what is new, check the following blog - What's New

Change can be hard, but the move was necessary. The above blog includes a lot of good information, but here are some direct links:

Asking and Answering Questions in the Community

What You Need to Know About Blogging in the New Platform

Get to Know Your Profile in the New Community Platform

 

The The SAP Business One Landing page is still very much a thing and have not changed much in appearance, you will feel right at home and find most of the familiar links there. If you were using the community before you will also be happy to hear that the SAP Business One Support engineers that were active in the previous community incarnation are still as active as ever in the new form. You know their names – Ankit Chauhan, Jitin Chawla, Nidhi Singh and a few more.

Hope to see you collaborating soon in the community!

entry.title: Support Spotlight: Best Practices when Re-installing in SAP Business One
entry.content:
In this blog post our expert Paul Shannon from the System team in SAP Business One Support, shares a few handy tips on how and why a re-install can be done and why in some cases it might be a good idea.

Many different types of software can face performance issues or data corruption over time. Depending on the specific issue, it can often be more efficient to perform a clean install than spend time attempting to diagnose these problems. This also applies to SAP Business One. Another advantage of a clean SAP Business One install is removing or cleaning up any remnants of possible previous installations which can solve multiple issues.

The blog covers re-installing the SAP Business One Client, we will look at the services, both in MSSQL and HANA environment and finally some documentation tips to help you along.

SAP Business One Client

The first component we will look at is the SAP Business One Client.

There are a couple of ways a corrupted SAP Business One Client may show symptoms:
- A user is working in the client when it shuts down unexpectedly
- Attempting to install the SAP Business One Client fails with an error.

If the client is unstable or shuts down unexpectedly, it may simply be an issue with the SAP Business One user rather than the actual client. The SAP Business One user preferences may be corrupted in some way. This can be resolved by copying preferences from another user and applying them to the corrupted user. This procedure is covered in the below link:
Copying User Preferences to Another User or User Group | SAP Help Portal

However, if the issue lies with the client itself, you can follow the below steps to rectify this:

1. Uninstall each add-on solution from your SAP Business One client.
2. Uninstall DI API using Add/Remove Programs in Control Panel.
3. Uninstall SAP Business One client using Add/Remove Programs in Control Panel.
4. Delete the directory %temp%/SM_OBS_DLL.
5. Reinstall client-side components in the following order:

a. SAP Business One client
b. Add-on solutions, if applicable

Note: DI API is automatically installed during the SAP Business One client installation.

In the event the client installation fails for some reason, we have further options. Firstly, we should ensure we have cleaned the remnants of the previous installation correctly and that the installation package we are using is not corrupt itself:

  • Remove the "SAP" folder from the path - "C:\Users\<user_name>\AppData\Local\"
  • Download the SAP Business One installation or upgrade package directly from the SAP Launchpad to ensure that the original package was not corrupted.
  • Copy the installation package to the machine desktop and run the installation from there instead of installing the package from the shared folder. This step can help to avoid any network or permission issues caused by a remote installation.

The next option we have is to clean any SAP Business One Client components in the Windows registry. Delete SAP registry keys under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE and HKEY_CURRENT_USER.

Important: Please keep in mind to perform a registry backup before any modification. Any registry modifications are done at your own risk.

For more information, please see
https://support.microsoft.com/en-in/help/322756/how-to-back-up-and-restore-the-registry-in-windows.

 

SAP Business One Services

Depending on whether you are running a HANA or MSSQL version of SAP Business One, we have different procedures for a clean installation of the services. However, in principle these have the same steps:

  • Remove existing components completely, manually force the uninstall if necessary
  • Delete or rename existing installation folders
  • Attempt a fresh install of all components

Important: Before attempting any of the below steps, ensure you have backed up your whole server if possible (HANA), SLDModel.SLDDatadatabase and Windows registry (MSSQL).

 

For an SAP Business One MSSQL environment we can first attempt an uninstall via Add/Remove programs (Control Panel → search for SAP Business One ServerTools → Uninstall).

If this does not work or for some reason you find that ServerTools is not listed in Control Panel, we have the option to manually force the uninstallation of the component by running cmd.exe as Administrator using the below command:

msiexec.exe /x {"ProductCode"}

You can find the correct product code of the component in the setup.ini file in the upgrade package (Packages → ServerTools → setup.ini file). Then backup SLDModel.SLDDatadatabase via MSSQL Server Management Studio before deleting it. From this point we can delete or rename the old installation folder.

Next, to be sure we have cleared out any remnants of the previous install completely, you can clean the registry. Again, be sure to back up the registry before this next step. Open regedit.exe and then search for "ProductCode" (Packages → Server Tools → setup.ini file). After these steps and before attempting the new install, reboot your machine.

In SAP Business One version 10 FP2111 and later, we have the option of the new “Components Wizard”. This new feature can greatly simplify this process:

  • Go to Add/Remove programs → Control Panel → search for SAP Business One Components Wizard → Uninstall.
  • Delete or rename the folder SAP Business One Servertools from "C:\Program Files\SAP".
  • To unregister or perform a clean uninstall the SAP Business One Servertools, select each component under \\..\\B1100_PL2111\Packages.x64\ComponentsWizard\MSI, right click and select Uninstall.
  • Reboot the machine.

It should now be possible to complete a fresh install using the Server Tools/Components Wizard installer.

 

For an SAP Business One HANA Environment it is possible to perform a forced uninstallation using the PuTTY command window. First back up the whole server if possible and back up the SAP HANA instance.

Execute the following command as root to list all SAP Business One related rpm packages:
rpm -qa | grep B1

If all the packages listed from the above step are SAP Business One related, use the following command to uninstall them all:
rpm -qa | grep B1 | xargs rpm -ev

Otherwise, it is possible to uninstall them one by one with the command:
rpm -ev

Next, we will need to open SAP HANA Studio. For SAP Business One version 10.0, all database administration tasks must be performed at tenant level (usually NDB@NDB) instead of System ID (usually NDB) level.

First, drop the schemas SBOCOMMON and SLDDATA by right-clicking and selecting "Delete". For SAP Business One version 10.0 FP2208 and later, also drop schema B1AS.

Drop the user COMMON by going to "Security"->"Users"->COMMON. Right-click and select "Delete". For SAP Business One version 10.0, also drop the below users:
B1_xxxxxx_RW,
B1_xxxxxx_RO,
SAPB1_DB_USER_xxxxxx,
B1_SBOCOMMON,
B1_SBOCOMMON_RW users with Restricted option.

Note: Do not drop the B1_xxxxxx_RW, B1_xxxxxx_RO users with CASCADE in case of any dependencies.

Delete or rename the folder /usr/sap/SAPBusinessOne and then reboot the server to release any ports occupied by deleted processes. Lastly, remove the SAP service control file, using the command:
rm -rf /etc/init.d/sapb1servertools.

We are now ready to launch the installation wizard to install server components.

To do that using PuTTY, go to the directory /Packages.linux/ServerComponents/ and execute the command:
./install

Documentation

For more detailed information on the above topics, check the SAP Notes in the below links:
2432304 - Troubleshooting Client Installation Issues
1989534 - Troubleshooting Guide for Corrupted SAP Business One Client Installation or Configuration
2706617 - UI Configuration Templates_Function, Definition, Errors and FAQ
2788059 - How to Perform Clean Reinstall of SAP Business One, version for MSSQL Server Tools
2538555 - How to Perform Clean Reinstall of SAP Business One, version for SAP HANA Server Components

Support Spotlight Series
We hope this read has been informative and beneficial. For more blog posts and tips from the SAP Business One Support team, please visit our space on the communities:
https://blogs.sap.com/tag/b1-support-spotlight/

For questions about SAP Business One, please submit your question here.

entry.title: Introducing Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud
entry.content:
Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud

If you’re working on SAP Business One in a Cloud environment, there is a good chance you are using Cloud Control Center (CCC) to manage the product's Lifecycle operations leveraging from its integration with Microsoft’s Active Directory service. Since CCC’s launch, the built-in integration to Active Directory service has served as the primary backbone for managing users access to SAP Business One in a secured and reliable manner.
With CCC’s PL 20, we’re taking it one step further by adding the option to use Identity Provider (IDP) services for signing-in to SAP Business One. By utilizing IDP services in CCC, hosting providers can offer their customers a seamless user experience by signing in to SAP Business One with their own IDP accounts (e.g. same Azure account they may use for MS365) and utilizing security best practices such as Two Factor Authentication.

Getting started

If you are familiar with Identity and Authentication management (IAM) solution in SAP Business One (Click here to read my latest blog on it), good news 😎 you are well on the way to mastering the setup of IAM also in Cloud Control Center as the configuration steps are almost identical.
To configure IAM in Cloud Control Center, follow the five simple steps below:

1️⃣  Register FP 2405 as a software repository in CCC

2️⃣  Activate “Enable Third Party Identity Provider” Global Setting

3️⃣  Add an Identity Provider and activate it

4️⃣  Add an Identity Provider user and bind it to company users

5️⃣  Sign-in to SAP Business One with an IDP user


Here’s a quick 📹 (no audio) capturing the steps above.


Take aways

Now that you got a hang of it, here are a few tips and tricks to take with you:

  • “Enable Third Party Identity Provider” Global Setting
    The new “Enable Third Party Identity Provider” Global Setting will be automatically visible in Cloud Control Center PL 20 once version SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2405 (or higher) is registered as a software repository.  
    The option to Sign-in to SAP Business One with an Identity Provider (via Open-ID Connect) is only available for Service units on version SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2405 or higher.

  • “Enable Mapping of IDP User to Active Directory User” checkbox
    A major advantage of CCC’s built-in integration with Active directory is its ability to automatically manage folder permissions and presentation server access for Active Directory users based on their Service Unit allocation in Cloud Control Center.
    To continue leveraging from this feature, make sure you tick the “Enable Mapping of IDP User to Active Directory User” checkbox during IDP user creation.
    With this mapping, a customer user can log into Remote Desktop Services with the Windows domain user account, and then Sign-in to SAP Business One client with the IDP user account.

  • Single Sign-On
    When working with the built-in Domain Active Directory as the identity provider, Active Directory users will continue leveraging from a Single Sign-On (SSO) experience as existed in previous CCC versions, under a new “Choose Company” window.

  • Partner Support User (PSU)
    To continue using the PSU functionality with IDP users, the Operator’s IDP user must have a mapping to an Active Directory User.

  • Customer specific IDP
    A hosting provider may setup Landscape Identity Providers (these Identity Providers will be shown in the IDP drop down list when adding an IDP user for customers across the landscape) in addition to customer specific Identity Providers (these Identity Providers will be shown together with the landscape IDPs in the IDP drop down list when adding an IDP user under a specific customer).

Documentation

For further reading, be sure to check out "Identity and Authentication Management in SAP Business One Cloud" How-to-Guide, walking you through implementation, usage and behavioral changes in SAP Business One Cloud when working with IAM.

I hope this Blog was useful to you as an introduction to the Identity and Authentication Management solution in SAP Business One Cloud PL 20. I look forward to hearing about your experience from working with IAM in SAP Business One Cloud, be sure to leave your feedback in the comments section below.

entry.title: Honey, I Shrunk the General Settings
entry.content:
If you wonder where the ‘General Settings’ disappeared in Web Client, this blog will tell you where to find it.

Starting from SAP Business One 10.0 Feature Pack (FP) 2405, the content of the ‘General Settings’ as you know it in the desktop client, is available in the Web Client, organized by the different modules.

This is part of a bigger restructuring of the Administration menu in Web Client that consolidates the settings and setup to be under the respective modules. The reason behind this change is that in desktop client, the configuration of a module is distributed across many forms in SAP Business One, some under General Settings, some under Company Details and so on - in Web Client, we’ve organized the configuration by SAP Business One modules (e.g., Inventory, Accounting, etc.) so you will find all related configurations in one place.

Terminology wise, Configuration = ‘Settings’ + ‘Setup’, where ‘Settings’ refer to values represented by checkbox, radio button and list of values, while ‘Setup’ refers to records of setup data, such as Item groups.

The configuration model in Web Client is illustrated as follows:

 

Sub-module is a logical split of the configuration module, related to a specific functionality.

The module and sub-modules are listed on the tree menu and the related Settings/Setup appear both as a menu entry (in the tree) and as the content itself in the main area across one or more tabs.

While in SAP Business One desktop client, Settings and Setup can be in the same window, in Web Client it is separated, i.e., setting and setup are in different menus.

This model is flexible and adjusted according to the module content – a module can have zero or more sub-modules/settings menus/setup menus, depends on the module nature and content richness.

Let’s see some examples – first example is for the ‘Payments and Banking’ module:

As can be seen, this module contains two sub-modules: Banking and Payments. Each contains only Setup records, such as Banks and Credit Cards. In this specific module there are no Settings menus.

 

A second example is the ‘Sales and Purchasing’ module:

This module contains no sub-modules; it contains three Settings menus and one Setup menu (Freights). The Settings menu is divided to several tabs on the main area for better usability. Many of the ‘General Settings’ items in desktop client that are related to documents are now available under this module.

 

Since some of the new settings menus in Web Client do not exist in desktop client, we’ve added new entries in the Authorization tree to support the authorizations for these forms.

The configuration menus are searchable (both settings and setup menus) and linkable – you can access the setup/settings page by using the ‘Define New’ option (e.g., from Business Partner master data, jump directly to Business Partner Customer Group to define a new setup record).

To conclude, the new configuration framework in Web Client consolidates all configuration elements under their respective module, offering easier orientation and navigation to configure your solution.

entry.title: Understanding the Relationship Between Inventory Levels and Sales Using SQL-Correlation Coefficient
entry.content:
What is the Correlation Coefficient?

It is a statistical measure that can be used to measure the strength and direction of a linear relationship that exists between two variables which include the inventory levels and the sales quantities we are interested in. The correlation coefficient is a measure that ranges between -1 and +1:

1 indicates a perfect positive linear relationship.

-1 indicates a perfect negative linear relationship.

0 Indicates no linear relationship.

When there's more stock, more products are sold. If on the other hand, there’s more stock, sales decrease.

Important why Question? Why do we Calculate the Correlation Coefficient?

Optimizing Inventory level:

To maximize the benefits from sales, companies have to consider disposing of their inventories wisely since correlation helps in determining the validity of inventory volumes. Findings about this are listed below: Optimal stock quantities: It can assist in determining optimal stock quantities thereby minimizing holding costs

Demand Forecasting:

Forecasting demand is useful for retailers since they rely on stocks as an indicator of impending sales; however, trying to forecast demand has been proven as always being a complex subject. For this forecasting to be possible, retailers will first need to segment their product categories.

Resource Allocation:

It would be better if businesses managed their resources wisely so that they always had what was most needed by their customers among other considerations.

Strategic Planning:

Using correlation analysis can help to make strategic decisions like when there are going to be promotions in terms of pricing, advertising and other adjustments in the supply chain.

Code Magic

 

 

--Setting up the Environment
DECLARE @Whse nvarchar(10)
DECLARE @FromDate datetime
DECLARE @ToDate datetime

SET @Whse = '100' --try to restrict to specific WH instead of overall for better results.
SET @ToDate = GETDATE()
SET @FromDate = DATEADD(MONTH, -12, @ToDate)-- For the Last 12 months

--Gathering Inventory and Sales Data

;WITH InventoryData AS (
    SELECT
        T0.ITEMCODE,
        T5.DocDate AS TransactionDate,
        T0.ONHAND AS InventoryLevel
    FROM OITW T0
    INNER JOIN OITM T1 ON T0.ITEMCODE = T1.ITEMCODE
    LEFT JOIN INV1 T5 ON T1.[ItemCode] = T5.[ItemCode]  AND T5.[WhsCode] = @Whse
    WHERE  T0.[WhsCode] = @Whse AND T5.DocDate >= @FromDate AND T5.DocDate <= @ToDate
),

--Sales Data CTE

SalesData AS (
    SELECT
        T0.ITEMCODE,
        T5.DocDate AS TransactionDate,
        SUM(T5.Quantity) AS SalesQuantity
    FROM OITW T0
    INNER JOIN OITM T1 ON T0.ITEMCODE = T1.ITEMCODE
    LEFT JOIN INV1 T5 ON T1.[ItemCode] = T5.[ItemCode] AND T5.[WhsCode] = @Whse 
    WHERE  T0.[WhsCode] = @Whse AND T5.DocDate >= @FromDate AND T5.DocDate <= @ToDate
    GROUP BY T0.ITEMCODE, T5.DocDate
)

--Combining Inventory and Sales Data

SELECT
    I.ITEMCODE,
    I.TransactionDate,
    I.InventoryLevel,
    S.SalesQuantity
INTO #CorrelationData
FROM InventoryData I
LEFT JOIN SalesData S ON I.ITEMCODE = S.ITEMCODE AND I.TransactionDate = S.TransactionDate

-- Calculate correlation  Pearson's R

DECLARE @N float, @SumX float, @SumY float, @SumXY float, @SumXSquare float, @SumYSquare float
SELECT
    @N = COUNT(*) * 1.0,
    @SumX = SUM(InventoryLevel),
    @SumY = SUM(SalesQuantity),
    @SumXY = SUM(InventoryLevel * SalesQuantity),
    @SumXSquare = SUM(InventoryLevel * InventoryLevel),
    @SumYSquare = SUM(SalesQuantity * SalesQuantity)
FROM #CorrelationData

-- Calculate the Pearson's correlation coefficient

DECLARE @Correlation float
SELECT @Correlation = (@N * @SumXY - @SumX * @SumY) / SQRT((@N * @SumXSquare - @SumX * @SumX) * (@N * @SumYSquare - @SumY * @SumY))

-- Display the correlation

SELECT @Correlation AS Correlation

 

 

Conclusion

Inventory levels correlate with sales, this can help a lot in managing inventory better. The use of SQL can be so useful when it comes to processing and analyzing big datasets quickly. Not only does the query we just discussed show how data analysis can be done with SQL but it also suggests a way businesses can change their stock levels based on how much they have sold before.

For readers who want to delve deeper into the Pearson correlation coefficient and its mathematical foundations, I recommend visiting https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pearson_correlation_coefficient. This comprehensive resource provides detailed explanations, historical context, and various applications of the Pearson correlation coefficient, making it an excellent supplementary read to enhance your understanding of this statistical measure.

entry.title: Summertime Freshness: New Upgrade to SAP Business One Localized Demo Databases Now Available!
entry.content:
We are glad to announce that the SAP Business One localized demo databases have been upgraded to the latest release 10.0 FP 2405 and are available for download.

Along with the upgraded databases to release 10.0 FP 2405, we provide recent transactional data to make your demos look fresh and tidy!

The localized demo databases contain a pre-configured SAP Business One environment in 39 localizations. They are based on the model company OEC Computers and are offered for both the MS SQL version and the SAP HANA version of SAP Business One.

Demo, learn, teach, and test SAP Business One with fresh demo environment!

Download the Localized Demo Databases for SAP Business One

entry.title: Making SAP Business One Web Client accessible externally using nginx as a reverse proxy
entry.content:
SAP Business One Web client facilitates core business logic and processes provided in a new user experience based on the SAP Fiori design concept wrapped with amazing built-in Analytics capabilities. With the increased adoption of the Web client and transition towards a hybrid work mode, comes the need to make the Web client available externally, outside the boundaries of the traditional office space.

If you made it so far, it probably means you are eager to get started and learn more about the required configuration steps to make your web client accessible externally. Luckly, this is exactly what this blog was made for.

In this blog I am focusing on the configuration steps in the latest On-Premise versions of SAP Business One (FP 2208 and higher versions) for making SAP Business One Web client accessible externally using nginx as a *reverse Proxy.  

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
*A reverse proxy works as an interchange between internal SAP Business One services and external clients.
All the external clients send requests to the reverse proxy and the reverse proxy forwards their requests
to the internal SAP Business One services.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prerequisites for secured (HTTPS) communication

Before getting started, we must verify that the right “ingredients” are in place to facilitate a secured HTTPS communication channel between SAP Business One components. Here’s the ingredient checklist:

  • Trusted Root Certificate
    To secure SAP Business One Web client (so browsers do not mark it as not secure), it is necessary to import a trusted root certificate for all SAP Business One services during the installation. The certificate can be issued by a third-party certification authority (CA) or a local enterprise CA

  • Proxy Server
    Set up a reverse Proxy (e.g. nginx or your preferred method) for handling external requests for accessing the Web Client. To allow reverse proxy to handle external requests and communicate in a secured channel with internal SAP Business One components, make sure that the external addresses of System Landscape Directory (SLD), SAP Business One authentication service and Web client are supported by the certificate.

  • Prepare certificates:
    Generate the server.cer and server.key files from your PKCS12 (.pfx) file using the OpenSSL library.
    Copy both files to the ${nginx}/cert folder on the nginx server (If the cert folder does not already exist, create it manually).

For more information on preparation of certificates for SAP Business One Services (e.g. SAP Business One Web client) and reverse proxy, please refer to this section in SAP Business One Administration Guide.

Configuration steps

💡  Please note that the following configuration steps are based on best practice using nginx Server as a reverse proxy for SAP Business One Version for HANA.  For more information on nginx setup please refer to this section within SAP Business One version for HANA Administration Guide.


Watch the video below (04:51) to learn how to configure your nginx server to make SAP Business One Web client accessible externally or follow the steps below.




 

Step 1️⃣  Prepare your nginx server

  1. Verify that server.cer and server.key files (see more details under prerequisites) were copied over to ${nginx}/cert folder on the nginx server.
  2. On the nginx server, under the ${nginx}\html\ folder, create a folder named as ControlCenter. On the SLD server, go to ${SLDInstallationFolder}/ServerTools/SLD/webapps, get the SLDControlCenter.war file. Copy and unzip the SLDControlCenter.war file to ${nginx}\html\ControlCenter
  3. Download “nginx_conf” configuration template based on your SAP Business One deployment type (links are available accordingly in SAP Business One Admin Guide or SAP Business One Cloud Admin Guide).
  4. Extract the content of nginx_conf to the ${nginx} conf folder on the Nginx server





Step 2️⃣ Configure your nginx server

  1. Define within “b1c_extAddress.conf” file, under “Component Configuration” section, the Internal address of the following SAP Business One Services

    ℹ️  In the example below, all services were installed in a “single box” configuration under the following host name:
    Internal host name: cnpvgxxxxx700 
    External host name: cnpvgvxxxxx15.b1cloud.smes.sap.corp

  2. In the next section (slightly below) you will be required to define a single External address for the services appearing under #service

    ℹ️ Using the example above, we'll input external host name  cnpvgvxxxxx15.b1cloud.smes.sap.corp with port 8443


    🤔 Don't worry about all components sharing same address and port; nginx will use the internal API name in order to dispatcher the request to the relevant service behind.

  3. For Web Client, scroll further down and look for the #webclient section.
    Use the same external server name with a different port number (443)

    ℹ️  Using the example above, we'll input external host name  cnpvgvxxxxx15.b1cloud.smes.sap.corp with port 443

  4. After performing this step, restart the nginx service to apply the configuration changes.
    Navigate to your nginx folder and restart your nginx service
    cd /usr/local/nginx/sbin
    Stop: ./nginx -s stop
    Start: ./nginx 

Step 3️⃣  Test Nginx Configuration

Before processing to the next step, at this point it is highly recommended to test if the configuration is successful by attempting to access SLD and Authentication Server using their external addresses:

SLD Address
https://<nginx server domain name>:<listening port number of SLD>/sld/sld0100.svc

ℹ️ Using the example from configuration above
https://cnpvgvxxxxx15.b1cloud.smes.sap.corp:8443/sld/sld0100.svc

Authentication Server
https://<nginx server domain name>:<listening port number of SLD>/auth

ℹ️ Using the example from configuration above
https://cnpvgvxxxxx15.b1cloud.smes.sap.corp:8443/auth 

🤔 make sure that both external addresses are accessible from the SLD server itself. If not, please configure a DNS entry for resolving to Reverse Proxy server address. Do not progress to the next step unless the tests suggested in this step passed successfully. 

Step 4️⃣  Configure the external addresses for SLD and Authentication Server 

When a Reverse Proxy is used, the address and port of the authentication server and SLD will be the same. 

ℹ️ Using the example from configuration above, we'll input external host name  cnpvgvxxxxx15.b1cloud.smes.sap.corp with port 8443 for both SLD and Authentication Server 


Step 5️⃣ Mapping External Addresses to Internal Addresses for Web Client

As a last step, register in SLD under “External Mapping” tab the required Intenal:external URL mapping for Web client.

ℹ️ Using the example from configuration above we'll input https://cnpvgvxxxxx15.b1cloud.smes.sap.corp:443






 📣  Note
After finishing mapping the addresses for Web Client (or any other component added for external mapping), it is necessary to restart the affected services on the machines on which they're installed. 


Having completed these 5 steps successfully, your SAP Business One Web Client should now be accessible from externally. Nice Job! 🎉

I look forward to hearing about your experience and best practices you may come up with during your configuration journey, be sure to share your feedback in the comments section below.

entry.title: ¿Qué pasa en Soporte SAP Business One?
entry.content:
En la publicación del blog de hoy, queríamos tomarnos un momento para compartir una actualización rápida sobre lo que hemos estado trabajando en la organización de Soporte de SAP Business One. Hemos estado bastante ocupados, con muchos cambios emocionantes e iniciativas en marcha.

Mantente actualizado y sigue leyendo para descubrir en qué hemos estado trabajando...

 

Webinars Interactivos

 

Primero, lanzamos una nueva iniciativa con sesiones interactivas y atractivas dirigidas por nuestros Ingenieros de Soporte para nuestros Partners de SAP Business One. En abril, lanzamos la sesión "Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte de SAP Business One como Partner". Ofrecimos esta sesión en cuatro idiomas diferentes: inglés, chino, español y portugués, en cuatro sesiones diferentes.

¡Estamos encantados con la participación de aproximadamente 250 asistentes! Está claro que hay un verdadero interés por este tipo de eventos. Por eso, nos complace anunciar que la inscripción ya está abierta para nuestra próxima sesión:  "SAP Business One Version for SAP HANA - Database Administration Best Practices" ("SAP Business One Versión para SAP HANA - Mejores Prácticas de Administración de Bases de Datos"). Realizaremos esta sesión en inglés el martes 25 de junio a las 9AM EST, 2PM GMT, 3PM CET. Algunos de los temas que cubriremos incluyen:

SDC (Sistema de Contenedor Único) y MDC (Contenedores de Base de Datos Multi-inquilino) y la diferencia entre los dos enfoques.

Tareas de administración y mantenimiento, enfocándose en las mejores prácticas para un rendimiento mejorado.

Orientación sobre cómo encontrar contenido relacionado con la base de datos SAP HANA, ayudando a los participantes a navegar eficazmente por los recursos disponibles.

El orador de nuestra próxima sesión será nada menos que nuestro Líder de Área de Producto HANA, Sagar Dixit. Fomentamos una discusión interactiva, así que siéntete libre de hacerle preguntas a Sagar durante la sesión.

¿Interesado en unirte? Haz clic aquí para registrarte en nuestra próxima sesión: "SAP Business One Version for SAP HANA - Database Administration Best Practices."

Y eso no es todo: mantente atento a futuras sesiones que abordarán una variedad de temas como Activos Fijos, B1i, Capa de Servicio y más. Todas estas serán impartidas por expertos en temas dentro de nuestra Organización de Soporte.

Si te perdiste nuestra primera sesión o te gustaría revisitarla, te tenemos cubierto. Puedes encontrar las grabaciones y el material de presentación en inglés, chino, español y portugués.

Solo recuerda: el material de presentación siempre está en inglés y es exclusivo para Partners.

Respuestas Guiadas

 

Desde nuestra última actualización, hemos lanzado muchas nuevas Respuestas Guiadas que puedes consultar.

Si no estás muy seguro de qué son las Respuestas Guiadas, no te preocupes, tenemos una publicación en el blog que explica todo sobre ellas. Las Respuestas Guiadas son una herramienta fantástica para ayudar con la resolución de problemas, ayudándote a encontrar soluciones aún más rápido. Consulta la publicación aquí: "Guided Answers – Assisted troubleshooting for even faster solutions."

Las últimas Respuestas Guiadas lanzadas incluyen: - Trabajando con un Informe de Auditoría de Stock, Changing the Valuation Method of Items, Electronic File/Document/Report Failed to be Added or Generated

El Soporte de SAP Business One ha lanzado hasta hoy 28 Respuestas Guiadas diferentes. Encuentra todas aquí: Guided Answers

 

Comunidad SAP Business One

Como habrás oído, la Comunidad SAP ha recibido una renovación muy necesaria y se ha trasladado a una nueva plataforma. Si quieres informarte sobre lo nuevo, consulta el siguiente blog: What's New.

El cambio puede ser difícil, pero la mudanza era necesaria. El blog anterior incluye mucha buena información, pero aquí hay algunos enlaces directos:

Asking and Answering Questions in the Community

What You Need to Know About Blogging in the New Platform

Get to Know Your Profile in the New Community Platform

 

La página de inicio de SAP Business One (SAP Business One Landing page) sigue siendo muy importante y no ha cambiado mucho en apariencia, te sentirás como en casa y encontrarás la mayoría de los enlaces familiares allí. Si estabas usando la comunidad antes, también te alegrará saber que los ingenieros de soporte de SAP Business One que estaban activos en la encarnación anterior de la comunidad siguen tan activos como siempre en la nueva forma. Conoces sus nombres: Ankit Chauhan, Jitin Chawla, Nidhi Singh y algunos más.

¡Esperamos verte colaborando pronto en la comunidad!

 

  • B1 support spotlight
  • B1_Espanol

entry.title: Web Client - User Defined Fields Management
entry.content:
With the release of SAP Business One version 10.0 FP 2405, users can now directly manage their User Defined Fields (UDFs) in the Web Client. This new functionality includes several capabilities that enhance the user experience:

Key Features

  • UDF Tile Display:
    • The Web Client now shows the current number of User Defined Fields in a tile, providing a quick overview of existing UDFs.

 

  • Filtering UDFs:                                                                                                                                         
    • Users can filter UDFs, choosing to hide or show specific fields. For example, you can filter to show only UDFs related to Marketing documents. This filtering option is exclusively available in the Web Client.

 

  • Managing UDFs:                                                                                                                                       
    • Users can create, edit, and delete UDFs directly from the Web Client. This streamlines the process and reduces the need to switch between different interfaces.

UDF Tile:

UDF Filtering:

Creating a User Define Fields:

Showing User Defined Fields (already exists in previous versions)

  • Row Level UDF: users can create UDFs at the row level, making it easier to add custom fields directly to document lines.
  • Header Level UDF: header level UDFs are placed in a new tab. Additionally, there is an option to move UDFs to specific locations within other tabs using code via the UIAPI framework.

Row Level UDF:

Header-level User Defined Fields are placed in a new tab. There is an option to move UDFs to specific locations within other tabs using code via the UIAPI framework. (UDF Move Sample)

Summary

From version 10.0 FP 2405 onwards, the complete functionality for managing and viewing User Defined Fields is available within the Web Client. This includes creating, editing, deleting, and filtering UDFs, making it a robust tool for customization.

For more information related to Extensibility, please review the Help Portal. Additionally, we provide an introduction video on User Defined Fields in the Web Client.

I hope you found this blog informative and helpful. Your feedback is highly valued, so kindly share your thoughts in the comments section below.

entry.title: Enabling SAP Business One for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Are you willing to enhance your SAP Business One offerings with unparalleled analytics capabilities to your customers?

Dive into our exclusive e-learning course available on the SAP PartnerEdge portal, designed specifically for SAP Business One partners. This course will equip you with the skills and knowledge to enable SAP Business One for SAP Analytics Cloud, allowing you to enhance your offerings and provide AI-powered advanced analytics solutions to your customers.

 

Why You Should Watch

There are growing requirements from SAP Business One partners to adopt SAP Analytics Cloud for their customers. Its affordability and robust analytical capabilities make it an ideal choice for small to medium-sized enterprises. However, the initial setup can be daunting due to the complex connectivity options. This e-learning course is here to demystify the process and provide you with the knowledge and tools you need to succeed.

 

What You Will Learn

  1. Introduction to SAP Analytics Cloud Enablement Resources:

    Begin your journey with SAP Analytics Cloud through well-structured learning paths. Discover the courses designed to help you familiarize SAP Analytics Cloud.


  2. Overview of SAP Analytics Cloud Data Connectivity:

    Understand the two primary data connectivity options: Live Data Connectivity and Acquired Data Connectivity. Learn the advantages and limitations of each to make informed decisions for your business needs.


  3. In-Depth Look at Data Connectivity Options:

    Dive deeper into the live data connection to SAP HANA and the acquired(imported) data connection from on-premise SAP HANA and MS SQL. Detailed steps for both options will be provided to ensure a smooth setup.


  4. Hands-On Resources:

    Get access to various resources including learning journeys, partner academies, demo contents, and trial systems to practice and refine your skills.

 

Configuration Overview for Data Connectivity

  1. Live Data Connectivity

     

    Utilize the live data in analytics models, stories, and dashboards for real-time insights.


  2. Acquired Data Connectivity


    Just Ask for Natural Language Query by AI, available in Imported Data Connection

 

Watch Now

Don’t miss this opportunity to unlock the full potential of SAP Business One data with SAP Analytics Cloud. Access the e-learning course now on the SAP PartnerEdge portal and take the first step towards exapanding your portfolio with SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

Watch Here:

Video: ​Enabling SAP Business One for SAP Analytics Cloud

Presentation: ​Enabling SAP Business One for SAP Analytics Cloud

S-User is required to access the links.

entry.title: SAP Business One upgrade path - upgraded!
entry.content:
What's changed in SAP Business One upgrade path ?

 

Spoiler
Did you know that it's possible to install the latest SAP Business One 10.0 release from scratch and perform a direct database upgrade from any release of SAP Business One 9.2, 9.3, or 10.0. ?

Now that I've got your attention 😀, I'm happy to share with you our first step (more to come...) in simplification of upgrade process for SAP Business One and SAP Business One version for HANA.

In this initial step, we simplified the upgrade path by taking the following steps:

✔️Officially support direct database upgrade to latest SAP Business One release from any release on SAP Business One version 9.2, 9.3, 10.0 on an environment which was installed from scratch on latest SAP Business One release.

✔️Unified the "Gate" Feature Pack (FP 2208) in upgrade paths that require multiple version jumps.
See Flow chart below taken from Upgrade Strategy Overview .


















SAP Business One recent overview notes have also been updated to outline the simplified up-to-date upgrade path. See below example taken from SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2405 overview  SAP Note 3446770 



💡
As a best practice, it's always recommended to review the Upgrade path information within the overview note to make sure you are up to date with latest changes.

Hope you find this information valuable for your SAP Business One upgrade projects, stay tuned for future announcements on lifecycle management enhancements, more to come... 😀

entry.title: Document Approval Email Notifications in SAP Business One
entry.content:
Creating an automated email notification system for document approval in SAP Business One using PowerShell can streamline your business processes and ensure that documents are approved promptly. In this tutorial, I'll guide you through the complete process, including setting up the database, creating a PowerShell script, and running it as a service.

Overview of the Process

We'll accomplish the following:

  1. Set Up the User Defined Table (UDT) in SAP Business One: A table to log email notifications that have been sent.
  2. Write a PowerShell Script: This script will run every 5 minutes, query the database for documents awaiting approval, and send email notifications.
  3. Create a Windows Service: Automate the execution of the PowerShell script by creating a Windows service.
  4. Testing: Ensure that everything works as expected.

Step 1: Set Up the User Defined Table in SAP Business One

To begin, you'll need to create a User Defined Table (UDT) in SAP Business One that will keep track of the emails sent. This will prevent duplicate notifications for the same document.

How to Create a User Defined Table

  1. Log into SAP Business One.

  2. Navigate to: Tools > Customization Tools > User-Defined Tables - Setup.

  3. Create a New Table with the following details:

    • Table Name: @Z_APPR_EMAILS
    • Description: Approval Email Logs
    • Type: No Object

  4. Add Fields to the Table:

    • U_email: Email address to which the notification was sent.
    • U_userid: User ID associated with the document.
    • U_internalNumber: Internal Document Number.
    • U_ObjType: Object Type of the document.
    • U_status: Status of the email (e.g., Sent, Failed).
    • U_sentDate: Date the email was sent.
    • U_sentTime: Time the email was sent.

Step 2: Write the PowerShell Script

We'll create a PowerShell script that connects to the SQL Server database, retrieves documents awaiting approval, and sends email notifications.

PowerShell Script: SendEmailNotifications.ps1

Here's the complete script:

 

 

# Main loop to run every 5 minutes
while ($true) {

# Define SQL Server connection parameters
$serverInstance = "<Database Server Name>"
$database = "<Database Name>"
$username = "<UserName>"
$password = "<Password>"
$query = @"
SELECT T0.[WddCode], T0.[DocEntry], T0.[ObjType], T0.[DocDate], T0.[CreateDate], T0.[CurrStep], T1.[UserID], T2.[U_NAME], T2.[E_Mail] as "Email"
FROM OWDD T0
INNER JOIN WDD1 T1 ON T0.[WddCode]= T1.[WddCode] AND T0.[CurrStep]=T1.[StepCode]
INNER JOIN OUSR T2 ON T1.[UserID] = T2.[USERID]
LEFT JOIN [dbo].[@Z_APPR_EMAILS] T3 ON T3.[U_userid]=T1.[UserID] AND T3.[U_internalNumber]=T0.[WddCode] AND T3.[U_ObjType]=T0.[ObjType]
WHERE T0.[Status] ='W' AND T0.[WddCode]=4 AND T3.[Code] IS NULL
"@

# Create SQL connection string
$connectionString = "Server=$serverInstance;Database=$database;User ID=$username;Password=$password;"

# Create SQL connection
$connection = New-Object System.Data.SqlClient.SqlConnection($connectionString)

# Create SQL command
$command = $connection.CreateCommand()
$command.CommandText = $query

# Open SQL connection
$connection.Open()

# Execute SQL query
$reader = $command.ExecuteReader()

# Process query results and send email notifications
if ($reader.HasRows) {
Write-Host "Query Results:"
while ($reader.Read()) {
$row = @{
WddCode = $reader["WddCode"]
DocEntry = $reader["DocEntry"]
ObjType = $reader["ObjType"]
DocDate = $reader["DocDate"]
CreateDate = $reader["CreateDate"]
CurrStep = $reader["CurrStep"]
UserID = $reader["UserID"]
U_NAME = $reader["U_NAME"]
Email = $reader["Email"]
}
Write-Output $row

# Send email notification
$to = $row.Email
$subject = "Document Approval Notification"
$body = "Hello $($row.U_NAME),<br><br>A document is waiting for your approval, "
$body += "please log in to the system to review and approve this document.<br><br>Regards,<br>SAP automatic notification"
Send-MailMessage -To $to -From "sap.notification@yourcompany.com" -Subject $subject -Body $body -BodyAsHtml -SmtpServer "smtp.yourcompany.com"

# Calculate the current time in hours and minutes combined into a single integer
$currentDateTime = Get-Date
$currentTime = $currentDateTime.ToString("HHmm")

# Calculate the current date in YYYYMMDD format
$currentDate = $currentDateTime.ToString("yyyyMMdd", [System.Globalization.CultureInfo]::InvariantCulture)

# Close the DataReader before executing the insert command
$reader.Close()

# Get the latest Code number
$maxCodeQuery = "SELECT MAX(CAST(Code AS INT)) FROM [dbo].[@Z_APPR_EMAILS]"
$maxCodeCommand = $connection.CreateCommand()
$maxCodeCommand.CommandText = $maxCodeQuery
$latestCode = $maxCodeCommand.ExecuteScalar()
$newCode = $latestCode + 1

# Insert line into the database
$insertQuery = "INSERT INTO [dbo].[@Z_APPR_EMAILS] (Code, Name, U_email, U_userid, U_internalNumber, U_ObjType, U_status, U_sentDate, U_sentTime) VALUES ('$newCode', '$newCode', '$to', '$($row.UserID)', '$($row.WddCode)', '$($row.ObjType)', 'Sent', $currentDate, $currentTime)"
$insertCommand = $connection.CreateCommand()
$insertCommand.CommandText = $insertQuery
$insertCommand.ExecuteNonQuery() | Out-Null
Write-Host "Line inserted into database for document $($row.DocEntry)." -ForegroundColor Cyan

# Reopen the DataReader after executing the insert command
$reader = $command.ExecuteReader()
}
} else {
Write-Host "No documents awaiting approval found." -ForegroundColor Yellow
}

# Close SQL reader and connection
$reader.Close()
$connection.Close()

# Sleep for 5 minutes before next iteration
Start-Sleep -Seconds 300
}

 

 

Explanation of the Script

  1. Connection Parameters: Establish a connection with your SQL Server database by providing server instance, database name, username, and password.

  2. SQL Query: Retrieve documents awaiting approval. This query checks if the document's status is 'W' and if no entry exists in the @Z_APPR_EMAILS table.

  3. Sending Emails: For each document found, the script sends an email using Send-MailMessage.

  4. Logging: After sending the email, the script logs the sent email details in the @Z_APPR_EMAILS table.

  5. Continuous Execution: The script runs continuously in a loop with a 5-minute pause between iterations.

Step 3: Create a Windows Service

Creating a Windows service allows your script to run automatically without manual intervention. Follow these steps to create and manage the service.

PowerShell Service Script

Here's the script to set up your PowerShell script as a Windows service:

 

 

# Define service name and description
$serviceName = "EmailNotificationService"
$serviceDisplayName = "Email Notification Service"
$serviceDescription = "Service to send email notifications for document approval"

# Define script path
$scriptPath = "C:\scripts\SendEmailNotifications.ps1"

# Create service
New-Service -Name $serviceName -DisplayName $serviceDisplayName -Description $serviceDescription -BinaryPathName "C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File $scriptPath" -StartupType

 

 

 

Step 4: Testing

  • Create a Test Document for Approval:

    • Log into SAP Business One and create a new document that requires approval.

  • Check for Email Notification:

    • Wait a few minutes and check the email inbox of the user responsible for approving the document.
    • You should receive an email similar to the one below:

     

 

  • Verify Log Entry in @Z_APPR_EMAILS Table:

entry.title: Unifying Your Business Operations: Integrating SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Business One
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) need more than just robust ERP systems to stay competitive. they need integrated digital workspaces that unify access to all their critical applications. SAP Build Work Zone, when integrated with SAP Business One, offers a powerful solution to streamline operations, enhance collaboration, and boost productivity. This blog post provides an overview of SAP Build Work Zone and its use cases for SAP Business customers, as well as the overview of this integration, concluding with recommended e-learning resources for those interested in exploring the details further.

 

SAP Build Work Zone Overview

SAP Build Work Zone is a digital workplace solution that offers a unified, role-based user experience, integrating applications, content, and processes in a single interface. It comes in two editions:

 

SAP Build Work Zone Use cases

For SAP Business One customers, where often only about 10% of employees use the ERP system, there's an opportunity for partners to extend their reach by targeting the non-ERP users and even non-Employee users with Work Zone solutions. Traditionally, these users have relied on third-party or custom-built solutions. In addition, SAP Work Zone Standard Edtion can complement other BTP apps and services, particularly since it is included at no additional cost with Build Apps and Build Process Automation.

The Advanced Edition is also a cost-effective option for replacing third-party groupware or intranet/extranet solutions.

This approach enables partners to generate recurring cloud revenue and additional income from contents and services.

 

Integration with SAP Business One

This illustrates the Build Work Zone demo site, featuring tiles and cards.

  • HR Space includes the Employee Self-Service page with two tile apps:
    • Employee Lookup app - built with SAP Build Apps
    • Employee Store Apps - 3rd party UI5 app
  • Sales Space contains two pages:
    • Overview Page features ...
      • Company dashboard - brought from SAP Analytics Cloud Story
      • Business Partners app - built with SAP Build Apps
    • Sales Processing Page is divided into two sections “Sales Orders” and “Invoicing”, each hosting ...
      • Business One Web Client URL app - opens SAP Business One Web Client Views
      • UI Integration card - built with SAP Business Application Studio with no-code manner. it displays the most-concise pieces of business data and enable straightforward and decisive user action in a limited-space container, helping users structure their work in an intuitive and dynamic way.

 

This site was configured with the view mode "Space and Pages – New Experience" to host UI integration cards. 

 

Then how to create a tile and card in a site? Here are three ways to manage business contents in a site.

 

Overview of Step-by-Step Process to create the above demo site in Build Work Zone standard edition, integrated with SAP Business One

Prerequisites

  1. Maintain a destination to represent SAP Business One service layer in BTP subaccount.
  2. Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication (IAS) to let IAS users work as business users across the different systems with single sign-on. 
  3. Optionally create applications in the SAP IAS admin console for SAP Business One if you want to achieve single sign-on with SAP Business One Web Client.

Configuration steps

  1. Create the Work Zone standard edition in BTP subaccount.
  2. Prepare for apps for Tiles and Cards for the above demo site.
    • Create applications in Build Apps and/or Business Application Studio and deploy them to HTML5 repository (the managed router created by Build Work Zone) in the BTP subaccount.
    • Create UI integration cards and build a content package for UI integration cards in Business Application Studio.
    • Create a SAP Analytics Cloud Story.
  3. Create a new site in the Build Work Zone Site Manager
  4. Create the 1st space for HR 
  5. Create the 2nd space for sales and its 1st page
  6. Deploy the content package for the UI integration cards to Work Zone and assign the role
  7. Create the 2nd page for the sales space, including UI integration cards

 

Conclusion and Call-to-action

Integrating SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Business One provides a powerful solution for SMEs looking to unify their IT environments, enhance productivity, and improve business agility. With its flexible capabilities, role-based access, and mobile readiness, SAP Build Work Zone is an essential tool for businesses aiming to streamline their operations and empower their employees and business partners.

For the detailed step-by-step guides on how to implement this integration and explore its full potential, access the e-learning course on the SAP PartnerEdge portal (S-user required) : 

  • Presentation
  • Step-by-step video
  • How to create UI Integration Cards exercise

entry.title: SAP Business One Upgrade Challenges from SAP B1 8.81 to SAP B1 10.00 release family
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I want to share my experience to upgrade the SAP B1 version from 8.81 to 10.00. In this blog, I want to give you an insight about the Challenges in the version upgrade from the database size point of view.

So, what Challenges we do have to face when upgrading the SAP B1 on MSSQL version
from SAP B1 8.0 release family to SAP B1 10.00 release family with condition as follows:
1. Database size >= 100 GB
2. Database size < 100 GB


They are as follows:
A. Duration to upgrade (from start time to end time).
For this one, these followings should be considered as follows:
1. Hardware specifications for SAP B1 live server
- we must find out the ideal hardware specifications because it will affect the duration of the upgrade. Of course, all users must stop to use SAP B1 during upgrade
- we must also see the HDD type and size of the disk space that is used to store the sap b1 production live database and backup file of production database of previous version. If not, the upgrade process will be
terminated and error if the disk space is not enough anymore. The impact is we must restart from the beginning

2. Software Specifications for SAP B1 live server
- we must use at least minimum version of software specifications and support sap b1 latest version in order to run the setup wizard
- Software specifications must still be under maintenance period

3. The upgrading stepwises in the Setup in Process of sap b1 setup wizard
- The detail stepwise seems unavailable because no information from SAP Global Support. For me, it is the main challenges for SAP B1 consultants if the end users ask how long to take place
and what kind of upgrades the wizard do.
So far I notice 3 main steps  as follows:
* Pre upgrade testing. This won't take so much time. About 5-10% from total
* Upgrading in the setup in process. This step really takes so much time. About 90% from total
* Finalization (summary of the upgrade result). About 1% from total

In the upgrade steps, sub steps are:
- Upgrade database tables, for example OIVL, OILM (this table upgrading takes so much time), TAX1, etc
- Upgrade non database tables, for example Upgrading Production Orders,Upgrading Sales Orders, etc

What are the message for SAP about this challenge? SAP must realize that they should provide better ways to upgrade the SAP Version so that the production server down time can be minimized.
The prospect customers or lead will concern about it.

Herewith this post,I have attached a screen shot to show the SAP B1 database version history by querying it in the SQL query and the login screen of SAP B1 to show that the upgrade is successful.

The db size after upgrade is increased to about 60% from original size. You can see this following picture:

So, I want you all know that it is possible to upgrade from SAP B1 8.81 to SAP B1 10.00 without upgrade to SAP B1 9.0 release family first but for the company database size is > 100 Gb, you must consider your hardware specification. 

Thank you and good luck

Sincerely,

Jimmy Michel

(member of SAP SDN (old) and SAP SCN Community now since 2007)

entry.title: Best Practices for Technical Implementation of SAP Business One Web Client
entry.content:
In the modern enterprise landscape, ensuring secure and efficient access to business applications is crucial. SAP Business One Web Client is increasingly becoming the preferred interface for users, offering a browser-based environment to manage various business processes. This article will outline the best practices for the technical implementation of the SAP Business One Web Client, focusing on key aspects like sizing, security, and availability.

 

1. Sizing the SAP Business One Web Client

Proper sizing is essential for ensuring that the Web Client can handle the necessary transaction volumes and throughput. SAP provides a dedicated sizing tool (S-user required) that considers the number of concurrent users and the underlying components such as the Service Layer, Server Tools, and the HANA database.

Key Considerations:

  • The web client itself does not significantly increase resource consumption as the user count grows.
  • The Service Layer and HANA database will require scaling as the number of users increases.

 

2. Security in SAP Business One Web Client Deployment

Security is paramount when deploying the SAP Business One Web Client, especially when exposing it to the internet. The risks associated with unsecured deployment, such as using self-signed certificates or exposing servers directly to the public network, include increased vulnerability to attacks and potential data breaches.

In the desired secure deployment, a reverse proxy is placed between client and SAP Business One server.

 

2-1. Solution Using NGINX as Reverse Proxy

One of the recommended approaches for securing SAP Business One Web Client is to use NGINX as a Reverse Proxy. This method is suitable for both on-premise and cloud deployments.

Overview of configuration steps:

  1. Install SAP Business One components with internal addresses and internal certificates
  2. Obtain a public domain name and a trusted certificate for SAP Business One
  3. Configure DNS records to link the public domain name with public IP address of the NGINX server
  4. Configure NGINX server
  5. Configure SLD in SAP Business One.

[Example: Configuration of b1c_extAddress.conf]

  • SAP provides the tailored NGINX configuration files from the SAP Business One Administrator’s guide - Section Configure Nginx Reverse Proxy.
  • Update the internal address and port for the services as well as the external public domain name and service ports to adapt these configurations to the customer's environment.

 

2-2. Solution Using Hyperscaler’s Service as Reverse Proxy

For cloud deployments, leveraging hyperscaler services like AWS Network Load Balancer (NLB) can be an effective way to secure external access to the SAP Business One Web Client.

Overview of configuration steps:

  1. Install SAP Business One components with internal addresses and internal certificates
  2. Obtain a public domain name and a trusted certificate for SAP Business One
    - Recommend to obtain a certificate from AWS Certificate Manager
  3. Configure AWS Network Load Balancer
  4. Configure DNS for AWS NLB for an external address
  5. Configure SLD in SAP Business One

[Example: involved AWS services for the solution]

In addition to the typical SAP Business One deployment on the EC2 instances, 

  • Configure a Load Balancer to act as a secure end point that routes external requests to internal SAP Business One services on the EC2 instances.
  • Recommend to use AWS certificate manager to issue the necessary SSL/TLS certificatefor the Network Load Balancer (NLB) at no cost. It’s goint to be renewed automatically too.

 

2-3. Utilizing secure endpoint for Service Layer

You can extend the above configuration to Service Layer in both solutions. then integrating SAP Business One with third-party and/or BTP applications/services becomes much easier.

[Example: Extend the configuration to Service Layer for the NGINX solution]

To extend the configuration to Service layer for the NGINX solution,

  • add one upstream block for Service Layer, specifying the internal address and port of the Service Layer
  • add additional location block for Service Layer, specifying the service path accordingly

For the hyperscaler solution, such as with AWS NLB, configure an additional target group for the Service Layer, similar to the setup for other SAP Business One services.

 

3. Availability and Load Balancing

Ensuring the availability of the SAP Business One Web Client is critical for maintaining business continuity. This can be achieved by deploying additional web client servers and configuring load balancing.

Overview of configuration steps:

  • NGINX: Use the ip_hash directive in the configuration to balance the load between multiple web client instances.
  • Hyperscaler's service: For example, with AWS NLB, simply add another EC2 instance hosting an additional Web Client instance to the target group for the Web Client in the load balancer. 

 

Operations and Maintenance

Maintaining the SAP Business One Web Client involves several ongoing tasks, including renewing domain names and SSL certificates before they expire. It's also essential to monitor the system's performance and scale resources as needed.

Key Maintenance Tasks:

  • Automate renewing a public domain name
  • Update the external certificate and internal certificate before expiry date.
  • Use SAP Business one setup wizard - reconfigure mode to renew an internal certificate.
  • Use third-party monitoring tools or services provided by your cloud provider to keep track of system health and resource utilization.

 

Conclusion and Call to action

Implementing the SAP Business One Web Client with these best practices will not only enhance security but also ensure that the system remains scalable and reliable. Whether deploying on-premise or in the cloud, careful attention to sizing, security, and availability will result in a robust and efficient deployment.

For the step-by-step guide on how to implement this configuration, access the e-learning course on the SAP PartnerEdge portal (S-user required) :

  • Presentation
  • Step-by-step video

entry.title: Unas cuantas actualizaciones de SAP Business One Support
entry.content:
Recientemente, hemos entregado una sesión sobre HANA y actualmente estamos ocupados preparando una sesión en el área de conocimiento del Integration Framework. Más sobre eso a continuación. 

Como esta es una nueva iniciativa donde estamos tratando de encontrar una forma diferente de compartir el conocimiento y las perspectivas únicas que tenemos como organización de soporte, realmente no sabíamos cómo sería recibida. La recepción ha sido buena. Después de 2 sesiones, hemos tenido más de 500 asistentes. También hemos recibido muchos comentarios positivos, tanto en las sesiones como por correo electrónico y en LinkedIn. Gracias por eso.   

En ese sentido, te animamos a que hagas oír tu voz; si tienes comentarios, positivos o negativos, no dudes en usar el campo de comentarios a continuación. Si tienes sugerencias para temas adicionales o preguntas sobre el material presentado, nuevamente, utiliza la sección de comentarios a continuación. Estamos escuchando. 

Próxima sesión: SAP Business One Integration Framework – Administración, Mejores Prácticas y diseño de escenarios. 

En la sesión, nuestro líder del área de producto Pavol Hrasko y dos ingenieros senior cubrirán algunas técnicas de resolución de problemas recomendadas para los puntos críticos más comunes, diseños de escenarios recomendados y tareas administrativas elementales para mejorar la fiabilidad del sistema. 

La sesión tendrá lugar el 24 de septiembre, de 14:00 a 15:00 CEST. Como quizás sepas, la sesión es para socios y es gratuita: 

 

Webinar: SAP Business One Integration Framework

 

Acceso a las sesiones grabadas 

 

Si no pudiste asistir o deseas acceder a las sesiones grabadas que se entregaron anteriormente, consulta a continuación. También tendrás acceso al material que se presentó. Ten en cuenta que aunque se hablan diferentes idiomas, el material siempre está en inglés. 

 

Sesión HANA: 

SAP Business One versión para SAP HANA - Mejores Prácticas en Administración de Bases de Datos (Inglés)

SAP Business One versión para SAP HANA - Mejores Prácticas en Administración de Bases de Datos (Español)

SAP Business One versión para SAP HANA - Mejores Prácticas en Administración de Bases de Datos (Portugués)

 

Sesión de Soporte: 

 

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Inglés)

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Chino)

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Español)

Maximizando tu Experiencia de Soporte con SAP Business One como Socio (Portugués)

 

¿Qué más?

 

Un breve recordatorio sobre nuestras Respuestas Guiadas. El soporte de SAP Business One ha lanzado hasta hoy 28 Respuestas Guiadas diferentes. Encuentra todas aquí: Respuestas Guiadas

 

La comunidad – estamos ahí, utiliza el conocimiento de los ingenieros de soporte y tus compañeros a tu favor.

Los Blogs Spotlight del Soporte – encuéntralos aquí: Consejos del Soporte de SAP Business One

 

Eso es todo por ahora. Esperamos verte en la próxima sesión.

entry.title: Cost Accounting Summary Report in SAP Business One
entry.content:
There were some questions regarding how to work with cost accounting in SAP Business One (B1), and that motivated me to write this blog. First of all there is some information in  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE/68a2e87fb29941b5bf959a184d9c6727/179c4757670a4c6586f3adf431bd412b.html.

Secondly, let’s try to define what is required for the cost accounting? You can find many different definitions for that on the internet. I choose the following one:

Cost accounting involves assigning costs to cost objects that can include a company's products, services, and any business activities. Cost accounting is helpful because it can identify where a company is spending its money, how much it earns, and where money is being lost.

So, as you can see, this is not a legal requirement to have the cost accounting, but it’s mainly used for the managerial accounting.

Once we are clear with the definition, let’s take a closer look at how you can define and work with that in B1. Under the Cost objects we use Cost Centre and under the Cost accounting we use Cost Accounting Reports.

This blog is related to the second part – cost accounting reports and I will dedicate it to the Cost Accounting Summary Report:

It can be that you may not see the Dimension dropdown list in your B1. Don’t worry, it means that you did not enable the dimensions under your Cost Accounting setup under the General Settings in Administration -> System Initialization:

You can see in this screen that once you have decided to use Multidimensions you cannot go back.

But let us go back to the report.

You can run the report with your own Cost Centres Hierarchy or using Cost Centres. I have defined the Cost Centres Hierarchy as following:

 And now have the following result:

In case you do not have a complex cost accounting structure you can use Cost Centre button. In the same data base with the same selection criteria, I have the following result:

 As you can see, the result is the same. However, the presentation is different. You can also print out the result using the system print layout or your own.

With the other filters like Project, GL Accounts, Add Journal Vouchers, Exclude Closing Balances JEs you can play with the results to understand your company situation and plan your activities to have the financial result better.

Display in Report part is common for both report presentations and is used in the other SAP Business One financial and accounting reports. However, if you run SAP Business One in the location with different currency displaying data in System currency can help you compare the results of different business locations.

This is an overview of the possibilities to use this functionality in SAP Business One. It should help to adopt your B1 to your business model to be effective.
In case you have used the functionality, I would like to ask you to share your feedback or thoughts in a comment or ask questions in the Q&A tag area.
